artifacts.content:
Mariana,

I’m lying here with the heating pad and just needed to write this out while it’s fresh in my head. Today’s pain in my lower back, right shoulder, and right wrist feels like everything from the last few years piled up in the same spots.

When I bent and twisted with that 40–50 pound box around 10:35 a.m. in the Edison High custodial storage room, it was the same kind of sharp pull I first started getting in late October 2023, after Dr. Nguyen at Community Regional Medical Center downtown told me I had those early degenerative disc changes. Remember how he ordered physical therapy twice a week for six weeks, and I only made it through the third week because the $40 copay each visit was killing us once your hours got cut at the clinic? I thought I could just work through it at Edison and be careful, but today it flared in that exact lumbar spot.

On top of that, the right shoulder and wrist pain is right where Maria Lopez at the Kaiser Fresno PT clinic has been working on me since July 2024, after I messed them up helping my dad move those engines at his shop in west Fresno. She warned it could turn into a chronic rotator cuff thing if I kept overdoing it. And it’s the same wrist I sprained back in November 2020 when I slipped mopping the Edison cafeteria on that overnight shift and Mr. Collins gave me that “informal warning” for not putting out the wet floor sign, even though I was the one who went down.

I’m trying not to freak out, but it’s hard not to think about how all these old injuries keep landing in the same places.

Love you,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Rough day with my back, shoulder, and wrist

artifacts.content:
Pain + stress history – write this down so I don’t forget details when I talk to the doctors.

Lower back (lumbar):
- First big scare was that October 2021 car accident in the Fort Miller Middle School parking lot when I was driving Sofia. Dr. Patel at Community Regional Medical Center said my lower back was already weakened from that.
- Then in May 2022 I took that unpaid week off work to help Mamá after her back surgery at Saint Agnes Medical Center. Lifting her in and out of bed at her apartment in west Fresno left that same lumbar area sore for months.
- Late August 2023, I messed it up again helping her move out of the old west Fresno apartment after her second knee replacement at Community Regional. Acute lower back strain with spasms since then.
- Late October 2023, Dr. Nguyen at Community Regional in downtown Fresno said there were early degenerative disc changes and ordered six weeks of PT (I only managed three because of the $40 copays and Mariana’s hours getting cut).
- Since February 2024, I’ve been seeing Dr. Hernandez at Fresno Spine & Pain Clinic for chronic lower back pain, with muscle relaxers and hydrocodone during bad flare-ups.

Right shoulder + right wrist:
- November 2020 slip mopping the Edison cafeteria overnight – sprained right wrist, got that informal “no wet floor sign” warning from Mr. Collins.
- Helping Miguel unload his semi at that Bakersfield warehouse last spring, same wrist felt sprained again.
- July 2024: hurt right shoulder and wrist helping Dad move engines at his west Fresno shop; ongoing PT with Maria Lopez at Kaiser Fresno.

All the same spots that lit up again at Edison on 12/5/25 and now 1/15/26.

Hi Carla,

Thanks again for checking in about my back and about Mamá. Things have been heavy, so I’ll just tell you straight.

Ever since early 2024 when Mamá’s kidneys failed and she started going to DaVita West Fresno Dialysis Center three times a week, I’ve been trying not to miss any of her sessions. Do you remember that Medi-Cal letter dated 2024-06-10 warning that if she skipped too many treatments they might review her coverage? That’s stuck in my head. On the morning I hurt myself at Edison, I kept watching the clock because I’d promised to pick her up by 3:30 p.m. for dialysis. I rushed through restocking in that narrow storage room instead of asking another custodian to help move the pallet.

My lower back has never really been right since late August 2023, when we helped her move out of the old west Fresno apartment after her second knee replacement at Community Regional Medical Center. That’s when I first felt the acute lower back strain and spasms. Then in May 2022, during the unpaid week I took off for her back surgery at Saint Agnes, I was lifting her in and out of bed at home and that same lumbar area got sensitive.

Now it’s the same spots again, plus my right shoulder and wrist acting up. Between the pain, dialysis runs, and keeping up with Sofia at Fort Miller and Lucas at Thomas, I feel like I’m running on fumes. But I’m glad Mamá still has you in Madera checking on her by phone.

Let’s plan a Sunday at your place once I can sit in the car longer than 30 minutes.

Un abrazo,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Mamá, my back, and everything else

artifacts.content:
Event: Weekly counseling with Ms. Alvarez – pain, panic, and money stress

Time: Wednesdays, 6:15–7:00 p.m.
Location: Community health clinic, counseling office
Attendee: Daniel Ramirez

Notes:

1) Talk about how the lifting and twisting at Edison High on 12/5/25 and again on 1/15/26 set off the acute lower back strain with muscle spasm and pain shooting into my right shoulder and right wrist.

2) Since the December 5, 2025 injury, sleep has gotten worse. I’m having more frequent panic episodes, especially at night. This feels a lot like after that 2022 side job accident when I almost lost a paycheck we needed for Sofia’s and Lucas’s school clothes.

3) Since last spring, Dr. Patel at the Fresno County Behavioral Health clinic has had me on sertraline and clonazepam for anxiety and panic attacks. In May 2025 he warned the meds could make me drowsy and slow my reactions. The night before the 1/15/26 injury I only slept about three hours, and I was still groggy when I picked up that box around 10:35 a.m. in the custodial storage room.

4) Also bring up the money stress: the $3,200 past-due car loan with Valley Credit Union, that 12/5/25 repossession warning letter, and the 20-minute call from the Edison break room the morning I got hurt. I rushed the restocking afterward trying to make up time.

5) Ask if it makes sense to keep sessions weekly instead of monthly for now.

Miguel,

Hermano, just catching you up while I sit here with the ice pack.

This morning at Edison, around 6:45 a.m., I was in the custodial closet by the gym lifting and twisting with those supply boxes again. As soon as I pivoted, I felt that same sharp tearing pain in my lower back with spasms, plus the pulling in my right shoulder and that sprain-type pain in my right wrist. It felt just like last spring when I helped you unload your semi at that Bakersfield warehouse when you were behind on payments and couldn’t afford extra help.

The thing is, these aren’t new spots. My lower back’s been touchy since late March 2023 when I hurt it lifting supply boxes at Edison, and Dr. Patel at Community Regional Medical Center told me it was the same lumbar area that got weakened in the October 2021 car accident in the Fort Miller Middle School parking lot when I was driving Sofia. Add to that the week in May 2022 I took unpaid to help Mamá after her back surgery at Saint Agnes Medical Center, lifting her in and out of bed at her apartment in west Fresno – same pain across the lumbar spine.

Now my right shoulder and wrist are acting up on top of that, same side Maria Lopez has been treating at the Kaiser Fresno physical therapy clinic since July 2024 after I messed them up helping Dad move engines at his shop in west Fresno.

I know you feel bad asking for help with the truck when money’s tight, but we’ve both been paying for all these lifts with our bodies. We gotta figure out how to work smarter, not just harder.

– Danny

Dear Dr. Hernandez,

I wanted to send you an update before my next appointment at Fresno Spine & Pain Clinic.

As you know, you’ve been treating me since February 2024 for chronic lower back pain, with muscle relaxers and hydrocodone for flare-ups. On December 5, 2025 at Edison High School, I aggravated things lifting and twisting with custodial supply boxes. Since then, the pain has been worse and now radiates from my lower back into my right shoulder and down into my right wrist, making it hard to sleep and do normal activities.

This isn’t coming out of nowhere. Back in late August 2023 I strained my lower back badly helping my mom, Rosa Ramirez, move out of her old west Fresno apartment after her second knee replacement at Community Regional Medical Center. Before that, in late October 2023, Dr. Nguyen at Community Regional in downtown Fresno diagnosed early degenerative disc changes and prescribed physical therapy twice a week for six weeks. I stopped going regularly after the third week because the $40 copay each visit was too much for us once my wife Mariana’s hours were cut at the clinic.

On top of the physical pain, the December 5 injury has stirred up anxiety. I’ve already been seeing Ms. Alvarez at the community clinic for counseling, and we just increased sessions from once a month to every week because the pain and sleep problems are bringing back memories of a 2022 side job injury when we almost lost a paycheck we needed for Sofia’s and Lucas’s school clothes.

I’d appreciate your thoughts on whether we need to adjust meds or imaging, and if there’s any safer work modifications I should push for at Edison.

Sincerely,
Daniel A. Ramirez

metadata.subject: Update on back, shoulder, and wrist pain after 12/5/25 incident

artifacts.content:
Marriage + stress check-in – for late-night talks with Mariana

1) Bodies carrying stress:
- January 2024 visit with Dr. Hernandez at Fresno Community Health Center: he wrote in my chart that my lower back (lumbar), right shoulder, and right wrist were all tight from stress when Mariana and I were arguing about bills and almost separating. Same exact areas I keep hurting at work.
- Those muscles light up again whenever money comes up, like with the $3,200 past-due car loan at Valley Credit Union. Their letter dated 12/05/25 about possible repossession had my stomach in knots.

2) Recent triggers:
- Morning of 1/15/26, spent about 20 extra minutes in the Edison High break room on the phone with Valley Credit Union collections trying to negotiate a new payment plan. I rushed back to the custodial storage room to make up time and tried to lift and pivot that heavy box instead of moving the pallet safely.
- I was already running on fumes from anxiety and the meds. Since spring 2025, Dr. Patel at Fresno County Behavioral Health has had me on sertraline and clonazepam for panic attacks. In May 2025 he warned they could make me drowsy and slow. That night I slept maybe three hours.

3) Family load:
- Mamá’s dialysis at DaVita West Fresno three times a week since early 2024; that Medi-Cal letter on 2024-06-10 about missing treatments still scares me.
- Meetings at Fort Miller Middle School with Sofia’s counselor last November about her behavior and grades, plus Lucas’s stuff at Thomas.

4) Main point: tell Mariana I’m scared the mix of pain, money stress, and family duties is burning me out, but I don’t want us to drift apart again like last year.

metadata.title: Talk with Mariana – stress, money, and pain
